query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "socrates: towards reliable automated evaluation of proactive llm media" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) by empirically measuring the relationship between LLM "memorization" (a canonical alternative vocabulary for the mechanism of interest in automated evaluation) and output quality/pathologies, which directly informs the reliability and evaluation metrics central to the user's question about "SoCRATES" and proactive LLM media evaluation.
